Entity_Description: Sunset Beach is a small waterfront community in Metro Vancouver, British Columbia, on the eastern shore of Howe Sound roughly 16 kilometres northwest of downtown Vancouver. Home to an estimated 424 residents, it sits along the Sea-to-Sky Highway (Highway 99) where the forested slopes of the Coast Mountains drop steeply to the Salish Sea. The enclave lies between Lions Bay and West Vancouver, directly across the water from Bowen Island, and is made up almost entirely of oceanfront and hillside residences reached from the highway corridor.

Short_Description: Tucked onto the eastern shore of Howe Sound, North America's southernmost fjord, Sunset Beach is a cluster of oceanfront and hillside homes strung along the Sea-to-Sky Highway between Lions Bay and West Vancouver. The Coast Mountains rise almost straight from the water here, so properties contend with steep driveways, marine humidity, and the heavy rain that sweeps in from November through March. Those conditions demand a {BUSINESS_TYPE} who understands coastal slopes rather than flat city lots. {COMPANY_NAME} delivers {SERVICE} built for this terrain, pairing local know-how with {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} on every job. Whether your home overlooks the water or sits tucked into the forest above the highway, our team brings {ESTIMATE_TYPE} and {RESPONSE_TIME} response. Ready to get started? Call {COMPANY_NAME} today at {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Sunset Beach occupies one of the most dramatic settings in Metro Vancouver: a narrow shelf of land on the eastern shore of Howe Sound, where the Sea-to-Sky Highway threads between the water and the near-vertical granite of the Coast Mountains. Unlike the flat delta lots of Richmond or the graded streets of the city, homes here cling to the slope or perch at the tideline, and that geography shapes every visit our {BUSINESS_TYPE} makes. Access is often a steep private driveway off Highway 99, and exposure to salt air and southwest weather is constant.

The community's building stock is almost entirely residential, a mix of older cabins and cottages that grew up as Howe Sound getaways alongside newer custom homes engineered for the hillside. West-facing decks catch the sunsets the community is named for, but they also take the full force of the marine climate. From November through March, heavy Pacific rain sweeps up the sound, and the constant coastal moisture encourages moss, algae, and drainage problems on exteriors, roofs, and retaining structures. A {BUSINESS_TYPE} who treats a Sunset Beach property like a downtown condo will miss what these homes actually need, which is why {COMPANY_NAME} tailors {SERVICE} to coastal-slope conditions.

Location also defines how we serve the area. Sunset Beach sits between Lions Bay, just six kilometres up the highway, and West Vancouver about ten kilometres south, with Bowen Island a short hop across the water. Because these Howe Sound communities share the same corridor, our crews plan routes along Highway 99 to keep {RESPONSE_TIME} response realistic even for a small, out-of-the-way enclave. We also serve neighbouring North Vancouver and the University Endowment Lands, so homeowners here get the same {SERVICE_LIST} coverage as larger centres.

For residents, that combination matters. Many suppliers and trades are back toward the city, so a {BUSINESS_TYPE} that already knows the Sea-to-Sky corridor, the tight access, and the demands of oceanfront property saves time and callbacks. {COMPANY_NAME} brings {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} of experience and {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} to every job, backed by {ESTIMATE_TYPE} so you know what to expect before work begins. From the tideline homes to the forested lots above the highway, we deliver {SERVICE} that respects how Sunset Beach is actually built and weathered.
Local_Facts:
- Located on the eastern shore of Howe Sound, Metro Vancouver's stretch of North America's southernmost fjord
- Estimated population of about 424 residents (GeoNames estimate, approximate)
- Roughly 16 kilometres northwest of downtown Vancouver
- Strung along the Sea-to-Sky Highway (Highway 99) between Lions Bay and West Vancouver
- Nearby communities include Lions Bay (6 km), Bowen Island (7 km across the water) and West Vancouver (10 km)
- The forested slopes of the Coast Mountains rise steeply from the Salish Sea, so most homes are oceanfront or hillside lots
- Mild oceanic climate with heavy Pacific rainfall from November through March and constant coastal moisture

FAQ_2_Question: How does Sunset Beach's coastal weather affect {SERVICE}?
FAQ_2_Answer: The eastern shore of Howe Sound gets heavy Pacific rain from November through March and constant salt-air moisture year-round, which drives moss, algae and drainage issues on local homes. {COMPANY_NAME} plans {SERVICE} around these coastal conditions rather than dry inland assumptions.
